explanation:
Basic **electronics** follows Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws, and Faraday's Law. Applying these formulas prevents costly errors and electrical failures.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the power formula with your component values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside acceptable limits.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guideline only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much power does my gaming PC need?
faq.a: Add your CPU watts + GPU watts + 10–15% for other components (motherboard, RAM, storage, fans). Then multiply by 1.3 for safety headroom.
80 Plus measures PSU efficiency at 20%, 50%, and 100% load. Bronze = 82%, Gold = 87%, Platinum = 90%, Titanium = 94%. Higher efficiency reduces heat and power waste.

faq.q: Should I buy a modular power supply?
faq.a: Modular PSUs have detachable cables, reducing clutter and improving airflow in your case. Semi-modular is a good budget option; fully modular offers maximum flexibility.

faq.q: What happens if my PSU is too small?
faq.a: An undersized PSU will cause system instability, sudden shutdowns, and can damage components. Always get at least 30% headroom above peak power draw.

faq.q: How long do power supplies last?
faq.a: Quality PSUs typically last 7–10 years. Brands like Seasonic, Corsair, and EVGA are known for reliability. Don't cheap out—your entire system depends on it.

faq.q: What's the best power supply brand?
faq.a: Seasonic, Corsair, EVGA, and Thermaltake are industry leaders. Look for 80 Plus Gold or better, and check reviews for your specific wattage.
